hype.text: France are massive favourites, which is wasted on Bell — self-appointed Baywatch hero, spent the entire winter indoors with his insect collection, couldn't name three of their starting XI if the £140 depended on it (it does). Facing him: Dom's Morocco, fancied by absolutely nobody except a bloke who discovered football in April and will be watching this one baked, quietly weeping about leaving Aberdeen.

Saturday night: Norway v England, and the entire chat is suddenly Norwegian — sorry lads, £140 is £140. Todd just needs Haaland to run England's back line the way Year 9 run him around St Mary's every day of the week, then he can get back to noodling on that bloody guitar knowing he's in the semis.

Darley's Belgium against Will's Spain — the London property expert versus a man doing a PhD on purpose. Spain should batter them, not that Will will notice, he'd genuinely rather be flinging himself under children on a slide in Prague. And if Belgium do nick it, Darley's missing the winner anyway because some girl walked past the screen.

2am Sunday kickoff and Ethan will actually be up for it — the first productive hour he's logged since the car finance went through. Argentina should see off Perez's Switzerland, but Perez does love committing to things he's never once looked into (see: buying a house in Sheffield), so he'll be backing his lads from his natural habitat: sat under a desk.

The whole syndicate's praying for an England implosion just to keep the sweepstake alive, but Ethan's Argentina are his only hope of making the balloon payment on that motor he can't afford — assuming he can be arsed to watch the kick-off.

Bell's spent six months muttering to woodlice in a dark room and now he's backing Les Bleus like he's Didier Deschamps' tactical analyst. Will's currently drafting a 40,000-word thesis on why sacrificing himself on a Prague slide was statistically optimal, so don't expect him to celebrate a Spanish winner until it's peer-reviewed. Two lads who actively despise football holding the tournament favourites is the most on-brand sweepstake stitch-up since Dave picked Qatar.
